message.content: You are a cross-tier-symmetry auditor (pass-5 priority). Audit /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay for pairs of PARALLEL mechanisms that implement the same concern but were hardened ASYMMETRICALLY (one got a guard the other lacks). Examples to check: BULK vs HIGH_STAKES reasoning tiers (timeouts, cancellation, retry, error classification); bootstrap vs pooled DB connection (GUC/session state); pipeline vs standalone runners (discover_and_confirm vs run_study; adjudicate vs adjudicate_with_baseline) — type guards, empty checks, uniqueness guards; confirm_whole_corpus vs confirm_unit_level; the unkeyed vs keyed provenance trail. For each asymmetry, name what one side has that the other lacks and why it matters. Return JSON: id (X-N), severity, file, line, title, description, poc, suggested_fix.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	"""Tiered reasoning seam — the single abstraction over LLM execution by stakes tier.
2	
3	No engine component talks to an LLM directly; everything routes through :class:`ReasoningSeam`.
4	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's hardened ``reasoning_client`` (ARCHITECTURE.md
5	§1, ADR-0003). Two execution tiers:
6	
7	- ``StakesTier.BULK`` — a local model runtime (loopback-only, e.g. Ollama) for high-volume,
8	  low-stakes work.
9	- ``StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES`` — a frontier model via a **fixed-cost subscription** path
10	  (the ``claude`` CLI / Agent SDK using an OAuth token). No metered API key is ever used:
11	  every metered Anthropic credential is scrubbed from the subprocess environment first.
12	
13	Hardened invariants preserved from the prior platform:
14	
15	- forward-proof credential scrubbing (any ``ANTHROPIC_*KEY``/``*TOKEN`` stripped; the
16	  subscription ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` preserved);
17	- hard per-call timeouts via a bounded worker pool with a saturation guard;
18	- independent retry budgets — exponential backoff for transient errors, long backpressure
19	  for rate limits — and permanent errors never retried;
20	- robust JSON extraction via a balanced-brace walker (not a greedy regex);
21	- loopback enforcement on the local backend (ADR-0003).
22	
23	Heavy backends (``langchain_ollama``, ``claude_agent_sdk``) and tracing (``opentelemetry``)
24	are imported lazily, so this module imports — and is unit-testable by mocking ``_attempt`` —
25	with none of them installed. Install the optional ``reasoning`` extra to run real calls.
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import atexit
31	import json
32	import os
33	import threading
34	import time
35	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
36	from concurrent.futures import TimeoutError as FuturesTimeout
37	from contextlib import contextmanager
38	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
39	from enum import Enum
40	from pathlib import Path
41	from typing import Any, Callable, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol, cast
42	
43	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
44	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_url
45	
46	
47	class StakesTier(Enum):
48	    BULK = "bulk"  # local model — high volume, low stakes
49	    HIGH_STAKES = "high"  # frontier model via fixed-cost subscription — gated, traced
50	
51	
52	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
53	class ReasoningRequest:
54	    prompt: str
55	    tier: StakesTier
56	    purpose: str
57	    params: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
58	
59	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
60	        object.__setattr__(self, "params", freeze_mapping(self.params))
61	
62	
63	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
64	# Errors
65	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
66	class ReasoningError(RuntimeError):
67	    """Transient reasoning failure (eligible for retry)."""
68	
69	
70	class PermanentReasoningError(ReasoningError):
71	    """Non-transient failure — never retried (bad model, auth, saturation)."""
72	
73	
74	class RateLimitError(ReasoningError):
75	    """Rate-limit / capacity signal — retried with long backpressure, not fast backoff."""
76	
77	
78	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
79	# Config (dataset-agnostic env contract, ASSAY_* prefix)
80	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
81	def _env(name: str, default: str) -> str:
82	    val = os.environ.get(name)
83	    return val if val is not None and val != "" else default
84	
85	
86	def _truthy(name: str) -> bool:
87	    return os.environ.get(name, "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
88	
89	
90	BULK_MODEL = _env("ASSAY_BULK_MODEL", "llama3.1:8b")
91	BULK_BASE_URL = _env("ASSAY_BULK_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:11434")
92	HIGH_STAKES_MODEL = os.environ.get("ASSAY_HIGH_STAKES_MODEL") or None
93	
94	BULK_TIMEOUT = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_BULK_TIMEOUT", "120")))
95	# Cap BULK generation length. ChatOllama/Ollama default to unbounded (-1); without a cap a
96	# local model can run away to context exhaustion, and the wall-clock timeout cannot interrupt a
97	# call already blocked in the backend (a leaked pool slot). Bounding output tokens is the real
98	# guard (LangChain's own ChatOllama example sets num_predict explicitly).
99	BULK_NUM_PREDICT = max(1, int(_env("ASSAY_BULK_NUM_PREDICT", "2048")))
100	HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT", "300")))
101	
102	MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_REASONING_RETRIES", "2")))
103	BACKOFF_BASE = max(0.0, float(_env("ASSAY_REASONING_BACKOFF", "1.5")))
104	RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF = max(0.0, float(_env("ASSAY_RATELIMIT_BACKOFF", "30")))
105	RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_RATELIMIT_RETRIES", "5")))
106	# run_json re-rolls on a PARSE failure. This is independent of the transient/rate-limit retry
107	# budget above (which applies per generation) so the two do not multiply into a retry storm; an
108	# overall wall-clock deadline bounds the total regardless (#102).
109	JSON_REROLLS = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_JSON_REROLLS", "2")))
110	RUN_JSON_DEADLINE = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_RUN_JSON_DEADLINE", "600")))
111	
112	# OAuth subscription token name (external CLI contract — not renamed).
113	OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV = "CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN"
114	
115	
116	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
117	# Credential scrubbing (ADR-0003 / no metered API) — pure, forward-proof
118	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
119	def is_metered_anthropic_credential(key: str) -> bool:
120	    """True for any env var the Anthropic SDK would read as a METERED credential.
121	
122	    Matches the *shape* — any ``ANTHROPIC_*`` var naming a ``KEY`` or ``TOKEN`` — rather than
123	    a static list, so a newly-added metered var cannot silently slip through. The subscription
124	    ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` does not start with ``ANTHROPIC_`` and is deliberately kept.
125	
126	    The match is case-insensitive (pass 3, #F-003): a metered key set under a non-uppercase name
127	    (``anthropic_api_key``) must scrub too — the AWS_/GOOGLE_ checks already uppercase, so this
128	    closes the one credential path that did not.
129	    """
130	    ku = key.upper()
131	    return ku.startswith("ANTHROPIC_") and ("KEY" in ku or "TOKEN" in ku)
132	
133	
134	# Env vars that — beyond metered credentials — would redirect the subprocess OFF-BOX or onto a
135	# metered provider, defeating ADR-0003 / "no metered API". The claude CLI honours all of these.
136	_UNSAFE_SUBPROCESS_VARS = frozenset(
137	    {
138	        "CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK",
139	        "CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX",
140	        "CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER",
141	        "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS",
142	        # TLS-trust / egress-interception knobs the Node-based CLI honours: a custom CA or disabled
143	        # cert verification enables a transparent off-box MITM proxy (pass 4, #G-018).
144	        "NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS",
145	        "NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED",
146	    }
147	)
148	
149	
150	def is_unsafe_subprocess_var(key: str) -> bool:
151	    """True for any env var that must not reach the high-stakes subprocess (#101).
152	
153	    Covers metered credentials, off-box/metered-provider redirects, proxies (which redirect
154	    egress), cloud-provider credentials (AWS_*/GOOGLE_*), and TLS-trust overrides. The ENTIRE
155	    ``ANTHROPIC_*`` namespace is scrubbed (#G-018): any such var configures the SDK's endpoint /
156	    auth / headers and could redirect off-box or onto a metered provider — the subscription token
157	    is ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` (no ``ANTHROPIC_`` prefix) and is deliberately NOT matched.
158	    """
159	    ku = key.upper()
160	    return (
161	        ku.startswith(
162	            "ANTHROPIC_"
163	        )  # whole namespace: endpoint/auth/headers/custom-headers (#G-018)
164	        or ku in _UNSAFE_SUBPROCESS_VARS
165	        or ku.endswith("_PROXY")  # HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY/ALL_PROXY/NO_PROXY (any case)
166	        or ku.startswith("AWS_")
167	        or ku.startswith("GOOGLE_")
168	    )
169	
170	
171	def scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
172	    """The process env hardened for the high-stakes subprocess (ADR-0003 / no metered API).
173	
174	    Every unsafe var (:func:`is_unsafe_subprocess_var`) is **overwritten to empty**, not merely
175	    omitted. The claude-agent-sdk builds the child env as ``{**os.environ, **options.env}`` — it
176	    *merges* over the inherited environment — so a dropped key would silently fall through to its
177	    inherited value (the metered key / off-box redirect would survive). Setting it to ``""`` in
178	    the dict we hand over guarantees the merge overwrites the inherited value (#101). The
179	    subscription OAuth token is preserved so the fixed-cost path still authenticates.
180	    """
181	    env = dict(os.environ)
182	    for k in list(env):
183	        if is_unsafe_subprocess_var(k):
184	            env[k] = ""
185	    return env
186	
187	
188	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
189	# Bounded timeout pool with saturation guard
190	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
191	_POOL_WORKERS = 8
192	_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout")
193	# Shut the pool down at interpreter exit (#F-043). wait=False avoids blocking exit on in-flight
194	# backend calls (already bounded by _with_timeout); without this, module globals can be torn down
195	# while a worker is mid-call, leaving behavior undefined. Mirrors checkpoint.py's atexit cleanup.
196	atexit.register(_pool.shutdown, wait=False)
197	_inflight = 0
198	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
199	
200	
201	def _release(future: Any) -> None:
202	    # Idempotent per future (#F-032 follow-up): decrement _inflight AT MOST ONCE for a given
203	    # future. The done-callback path and the interrupt-window fallback in _submit_bounded can BOTH
204	    # reach _release for the same future when add_done_callback appended the callback and then was
205	    # interrupted before returning — without this guard that double-decrements and over-releases a
206	    # slot (the inverse of the leak being fixed). A per-future flag set under the lock makes the
207	    # "_release runs at most once" claim true rather than aspirational.
208	    global _inflight
209	    with _inflight_lock:
210	        if getattr(future, "_assay_released", False):
211	            return
212	        try:
213	            future._assay_released = True
214	        except (AttributeError, TypeError):  # pragma: no cover - Future allows attrs; defensive
215	            pass
216	        _inflight -= 1
217	
218	
219	def _submit_bounded(fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> Any:
220	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if every worker slot is already in flight
221	    (hung backend calls would otherwise leak all slots)."""
222	    global _inflight
223	    with _inflight_lock:
224	        if _inflight >= _POOL_WORKERS:
225	            # Saturation is a TRANSIENT condition — surface a retryable backpressure signal so
226	            # _run_with_retries waits and re-tries rather than failing the caller permanently
227	            # (#104). Concurrent callers thus queue briefly instead of erroring out.
228	            raise RateLimitError(
229	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_POOL_WORKERS} in-flight) — "
230	                "backpressure; retry shortly"
231	            )
232	        _inflight += 1
233	    try:
234	        future = _pool.submit(fn)
235	    except RuntimeError as exc:  # pool shut down at interpreter exit — normalize, don't leak (#116)
236	        with _inflight_lock:
237	            _inflight -= 1
238	        raise PermanentReasoningError(f"reasoning pool is shut down: {exc}") from exc
239	    except BaseException:
240	        with _inflight_lock:
241	            _inflight -= 1
242	        raise
243	    # Register the slot-release callback under its own guard (#F-032): if a BaseException
244	    # (KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit) fires while attaching the callback, release the slot
245	    # explicitly so a permanent leak can't accumulate and brick the pool with spurious
246	    # RateLimitErrors. If the interrupt landed AFTER add_done_callback already appended the
247	    # callback (so it will still fire on completion), the explicit _release here and the later
248	    # callback both target this future — _release is per-future idempotent, so the slot is
249	    # released exactly once either way.
250	    try:
251	        future.add_done_callback(_release)
252	    except BaseException:
253	        _release(future)
254	        raise
255	    return future
256	
257	
258	def _with_timeout(fn: Callable[[], Any], timeout: float, what: str) -> Any:
259	    """Run a sync callable with a hard wall-clock timeout via the bounded pool."""
260	    future = _submit_bounded(fn)
261	    try:
262	        return future.result(timeout=timeout)
263	    except FuturesTimeout as exc:
264	        # No future.cancel(): a ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread already blocked
265	        # in a backend call. The worker keeps its slot until the call returns; the bounded
266	        # pool's saturation guard is the real protection against leaked/hung slots.
267	        raise ReasoningError(f"{what} timed out after {timeout:.0f}s") from exc

270	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
271	# JSON extraction — balanced-brace walker (robust to fences / trailing prose)
272	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
273	def _first_balanced_json(text: str) -> str | None:
274	    """Return the first balanced ``{...}`` or ``[...]`` span, respecting string literals.
275	
276	    A greedy regex grabs from the first brace to the LAST brace, mangling replies with
277	    multiple JSON values or braces in trailing prose; this walks the structure honestly.
278	    """
279	    start = None
280	    opener = closer = ""
281	    depth = 0
282	    in_string = False
283	    escape = False
284	    for i, ch in enumerate(text):
285	        if start is None:
286	            if ch in "{[":
287	                start, opener = i, ch
288	                closer = "}" if ch == "{" else "]"
289	                depth = 1
290	            continue
291	        if in_string:
292	            if escape:
293	                escape = False
294	            elif ch == "\\":
295	                escape = True
296	            elif ch == '"':
297	                in_string = False
298	            continue
299	        if ch == '"':
300	            in_string = True
301	        elif ch == opener:
302	            depth += 1
303	        elif ch == closer:
304	            depth -= 1
305	            if depth == 0:
306	                return text[start : i + 1]
307	    return None
308	
309	
310	def extract_json(text: str) -> Any:
311	    """Parse JSON from a model reply (handles code fences and surrounding prose)."""
312	    span = _first_balanced_json(text)
313	    if span is None:
314	        raise ReasoningError("no JSON object/array found in reply")
315	    try:
316	        return json.loads(span)
317	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
318	        raise ReasoningError(f"reply contained malformed JSON: {exc}") from exc

324	def _bulk_complete(
325	    prompt: str,
326	    system: str | None,
327	    temperature: float,
328	    model: str,
329	    json_mode: bool = False,
330	    *,
331	    seed: int | None = None,
332	    json_schema: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
333	) -> str:
334	    """Tier: BULK — local model runtime (loopback-enforced).
335	
336	    ``num_predict`` is always set (bounded generation). For JSON, a caller-supplied
337	    ``json_schema`` uses Ollama's schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path); otherwise
338	    ``json_mode`` falls back to loose ``format="json"``. ``seed`` lets a retry force a different
339	    deterministic decode without sharpening temperature.
340	    """
341	    require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
342	    try:
343	        from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
344	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
345	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
346	            "bulk tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (langchain-ollama) — not installed"
347	        ) from exc
348	
349	    # BULK's INNER bound (#G-003): the sync-tier counterpart to HIGH_STAKES's anyio.fail_after.
350	    # A ThreadPoolExecutor worker blocked in client.invoke() cannot be interrupted by the outer
351	    # _with_timeout, so the HTTP client timeout is what actually frees the slot on a hung backend.
352	    # A bare float sets EVERY httpx phase (connect/read/write/pool) to BULK_TIMEOUT, so a slow
353	    # connect THEN a slow read could total ~2*BULK_TIMEOUT — past the outer BULK_TIMEOUT+10 bound,
354	    # leaking the slot. Use an explicit Timeout so the TOTAL stays ~BULK_TIMEOUT (+ a small connect)
355	    # and aligns with the outer headroom. Fall back to the float if httpx is absent.
356	    try:
357	        import httpx
358	
359	        _bulk_timeout: Any = httpx.Timeout(
360	            BULK_TIMEOUT, connect=min(10.0, BULK_TIMEOUT), pool=min(10.0, BULK_TIMEOUT)
361	        )
362	    except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover - httpx ships with the reasoning extra
363	        _bulk_timeout = BULK_TIMEOUT
364	    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = dict(
365	        model=model,
366	        base_url=BULK_BASE_URL,
367	        temperature=temperature,
368	        num_predict=BULK_NUM_PREDICT,  # bound generation length (H1)
369	        client_kwargs={"timeout": _bulk_timeout},
370	    )
371	    if seed is not None:
372	        kwargs["seed"] = seed
373	    if json_schema is not None:
374	        kwargs["format"] = json_schema  # Ollama schema-constrained decoding (reliable JSON)
375	    elif json_mode:
376	        kwargs["format"] = "json"  # loose native JSON decoding (syntactic only)
377	    client = ChatOllama(**kwargs)
378	    messages: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
379	    if system:
380	        messages.append(("system", system))
381	    messages.append(("human", prompt))
382	    try:
383	        reply = client.invoke(messages)
384	    except Exception as exc:
385	        # Prefer the typed HTTP status (ollama.ResponseError / httpx carry one): 404 = the model
386	        # isn't pulled → permanent. Fall back to substring matching only if no status is exposed
387	        # (robust to upstream message-wording changes — audit M4).
388	        status = getattr(exc, "status_code", None) or getattr(
389	            getattr(exc, "response", None), "status_code", None
390	        )
391	        msg = str(exc).lower()
392	        permanent = status == 404 or any(
393	            s in msg for s in ("not found", "no such model", "try pulling")
394	        )
395	        if permanent:
396	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"bulk model {model!r} not available: {exc}") from exc
397	        raise ReasoningError(f"bulk tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
398	    content = getattr(reply, "content", reply)
399	    text = content if isinstance(content, str) else str(content)
400	    text = text.strip()
401	    if not text:  # an empty reply is not a valid result — retry, don't return "" (issue #5/R5)
402	        raise ReasoningError("bulk tier returned an empty reply")
403	    return text

414	def _high_stakes_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, model: str | None) -> str:
415	    """Tier: HIGH_STAKES — frontier model via subscription CLI/Agent SDK (no metered key)."""
416	    try:
417	        import anyio
418	        from claude_agent_sdk import (
419	            AssistantMessage,
420	            ClaudeAgentOptions,
421	            ResultMessage,
422	            TextBlock,
423	            query,
424	        )
425	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
426	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
427	            "high-stakes tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (claude-agent-sdk) — not installed"
428	        ) from exc
429	    if not _high_stakes_auth_present():
430	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
431	            f"high-stakes tier requires subscription auth ({OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV} or a stored "
432	            "~/.claude/.credentials.json); none found"
433	        )
434	
435	    options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
436	        system_prompt=system,
437	        model=model,
438	        permission_mode="dontAsk",
439	        setting_sources=[],
440	        allowed_tools=[],
441	        env=scrubbed_env(),  # ADR-0003: never pass a metered Anthropic credential
442	    )
443	
444	    parts: list[str] = []
445	    result_error: dict[str, Any] | None = None
446	
447	    async def _drain() -> None:
448	        # The CLI/SDK signals rate windows and API failures by COMPLETING the stream
449	        # (a RateLimitEvent, or a terminal ResultMessage with is_error) — NOT by raising.
450	        # We must inspect the stream, not just catch exceptions (issue #R4).
451	        #
452	        # Inner cancellation (#127): wrap the stream in anyio.fail_after so a hung subprocess is
453	        # actually CANCELLED (the cancellation propagates into query() and tears down the child),
454	        # freeing the bounded-pool worker. The outer _with_timeout only raises in the caller; it
455	        # cannot cancel this thread, so without this a hung HIGH_STAKES call would leak its slot
456	        # permanently. Fires before the outer wall-clock (HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30).
457	        nonlocal result_error
458	        with anyio.fail_after(HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT):
459	            await _drain_stream()
460	
461	    async def _drain_stream() -> None:
462	        nonlocal result_error
463	        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=options):
464	            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":
465	                rl = getattr(getattr(msg, "rate_limit_info", None), "status", None)
466	                if rl == "rejected":
467	                    result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "RateLimitEvent rejected"}
468	                continue
469	            if getattr(msg, "status", None) == "rejected":
470	                result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "rejected"}
471	                continue
472	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
473	                for block in getattr(msg, "content", []) or []:
474	                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
475	                        parts.append(block.text)
476	            elif isinstance(msg, ResultMessage):
477	                # Do not let a trailing ResultMessage clobber an already-captured 429.
478	                if getattr(msg, "is_error", False) and not (
479	                    result_error and result_error.get("status") == 429
480	                ):
481	                    result_error = {
482	                        "status": getattr(msg, "api_error_status", None),
483	                        "detail": getattr(msg, "errors", None) or getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None),
484	                    }
485	
486	    try:
487	        anyio.run(_drain)
488	    except Exception as exc:
489	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
490	
491	    if result_error is not None:
492	        status = result_error.get("status")
493	        detail = result_error.get("detail") or status
494	        if status in (429, 529):
495	            raise RateLimitError(f"high-stakes tier rate window ({status}): {detail}")
496	        if status in (401, 403):
497	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier auth failed ({status}): {detail}")
498	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier reported an error (status={status}): {detail}")
499	
500	    text = "".join(parts).strip()
501	    if not text:  # empty frontier reply is not a valid result — retry (issue #R5)
502	        raise ReasoningError("high-stakes tier returned an empty reply")
503	    return text

507	    """Route one request to its tier backend (no retry here)."""
508	    if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
509	        raise ReasoningError("reasoning is disabled (ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING)")
510	    p = request.params
511	    system = p.get("system")
512	    temperature = float(p.get("temperature", 0.0))
513	    model = p.get("model")
514	    json_mode = bool(p.get("_json_mode", False))
515	    seed = p.get("_seed")
516	    json_schema = p.get("json_schema")  # optional caller schema → constrained JSON decoding
517	    if request.tier is StakesTier.BULK:
518	        return cast(
519	            str,
520	            _with_timeout(
521	                lambda: _bulk_complete(
522	                    request.prompt,
523	                    system,
524	                    temperature,
525	                    model or BULK_MODEL,
526	                    json_mode,
527	                    seed=int(seed) if seed is not None else None,
528	                    json_schema=json_schema,
529	                ),
530	                BULK_TIMEOUT + 10,
531	                "bulk tier",
532	            ),
533	        )
534	    if request.tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
535	        # The high-stakes tier runs the Claude CLI subprocess (not the in-process Anthropic SDK,
536	        # so no auto-instrumentor covers it) — emit an explicit OpenInference LLM span so Phoenix
537	        # records the model/provider for this otherwise-untraced tier (#2).
538	        llm_attrs = {
539	            "llm.provider": "anthropic",
540	            "llm.system": "anthropic",
541	            "llm.model_name": str(model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL or "subscription-default"),
542	        }
543	        with _span("reasoning.high_stakes", llm_attrs, kind="LLM"):
544	            return cast(
545	                str,
546	                _with_timeout(
547	                    lambda: _high_stakes_complete(
548	                        request.prompt, system, model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL
549	                    ),
550	                    HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30,
551	                    "high-stakes tier",
552	                ),
553	            )

557	def _sleep_bounded(secs: float, deadline: float | None) -> None:
558	    """Sleep ``secs``, but never past ``deadline`` — so backoff can't blow an overall budget.
559	
560	    If the deadline has already passed, raise instead of sleeping the full interval, so a long
561	    rate-limit backoff cannot keep a caller blocked well beyond its deadline (#102).
562	    """
563	    if deadline is not None:
564	        remaining = deadline - time.monotonic()
565	        if remaining <= 0:
566	            raise ReasoningError("reasoning deadline exceeded during backoff")
567	        secs = min(secs, remaining)
568	    time.sleep(secs)
569	
570	
571	def _run_with_retries(request: ReasoningRequest, *, deadline: float | None = None) -> str:
572	    """Execute with independent transient and rate-limit retry budgets.
573	
574	    ``deadline`` (monotonic seconds) bounds both the cumulative backoff AND entry into each
575	    retry: once the deadline has passed, no further ``_attempt`` is started (#102, #F-022). It
576	    does not interrupt an attempt already in flight — that is bounded by the per-call timeout —
577	    so total wall-clock is at most ``deadline`` plus one in-flight attempt's timeout, not
578	    ``deadline`` plus the full retry budget of per-call timeouts.
579	    """
580	    transient = 0
581	    rate = 0
582	    attempted = False
583	    while True:
584	        # Gate retry ENTRY on the deadline (#F-022): the first attempt always runs, but a retry is
585	        # not started once the deadline is past — otherwise the per-attempt timeouts could stack
586	        # well beyond the overall budget even though the backoff sleeps were bounded.
587	        if attempted and deadline is not None and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
588	            raise ReasoningError("reasoning deadline exceeded before retry")
589	        attempted = True
590	        try:
591	            return _attempt(request)
592	        except PermanentReasoningError:
593	            raise
594	        except RateLimitError:
595	            if rate >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES:
596	                raise
597	            _sleep_bounded(RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF * (rate + 1), deadline)
598	            rate += 1
599	        except ReasoningError:
600	            if transient >= MAX_RETRIES:
601	                raise
602	            _sleep_bounded(BACKOFF_BASE**transient, deadline)
603	            transient += 1

616	@contextmanager
617	def _span(name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any], *, kind: str = "AGENT") -> Iterator[None]:
618	    """Emit an OpenTelemetry span if a provider is registered; a cheap no-op otherwise.
619	
620	    Stamps the OpenInference ``openinference.span.kind`` (default ``AGENT`` — a reasoning call)
621	    so Phoenix classifies the span instead of rendering it UNKNOWN. OpenTelemetry is imported
622	    lazily, so the seam traces when the (self-hosted, on-box) observability stack is wired and is
623	    a silent no-op in tests / offline (ADR-0003).
624	    """
625	    try:
626	        from opentelemetry import trace
627	    except ImportError:
628	        yield
629	        return
630	    tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine.reasoning")
631	    # start_as_current_span auto-records exceptions + sets ERROR status by default (#F-005 was a
632	    # false positive — see assay_engine.observability.tracing for the verification note).
633	    with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as span:
634	        span.set_attribute("openinference.span.kind", kind)
635	        for k, v in attributes.items():
636	            span.set_attribute(k, v)
637	        yield

640	class TieredReasoningSeam:
641	    """Concrete tiered seam. ``run`` returns text; ``run_json`` parses a JSON reply.
642	
643	    Backends and tracing are resolved lazily, so an instance constructs with no external
644	    dependencies; calls fail loud (``PermanentReasoningError``) if a tier's backend or its
645	    subscription auth is absent. When the observability stack is present, each call emits an
646	    OpenTelemetry span; otherwise tracing is a no-op.
647	    """
648	
649	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
650	        attrs = {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose}
651	        with _span("reasoning.run", attrs):
652	            return _run_with_retries(request)
653	
654	    def run_json(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> Any:
655	        """Generate and parse JSON, re-rolling on parse failure.
656	
657	        If the caller supplies ``params["json_schema"]``, the BULK tier uses Ollama's
658	        schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path). Otherwise it falls back to loose JSON
659	        mode + prompt instruction. BULK decoding can be deterministic (temp 0), so re-issuing the
660	        identical prompt would yield a byte-identical bad reply; each retry **varies the seed**
661	        (forcing a different deterministic decode) and *monotonically* raises temperature toward
662	        1.0 — never lowering it, so re-rolls never sharpen back toward the same output. ``params``
663	        is frozen, so each attempt builds a fresh request (issue #R6).
664	        """
665	        base_temp = float(request.params.get("temperature", 0.0))
666	        system = request.params.get("system")
667	        json_system = (
668	            f"{system}\n\nRespond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
669	            if system
670	            else "Respond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
671	        )
672	        last: ReasoningError | None = None
673	        deadline = time.monotonic() + RUN_JSON_DEADLINE  # overall bound across all re-rolls (#102)
674	        with _span(
675	            "reasoning.run_json",
676	            {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose},
677	        ):
678	            for attempt in range(JSON_REROLLS + 1):
679	                if attempt > 0 and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
680	                    raise last or ReasoningError("run_json exceeded its overall deadline")
681	                temp = min(1.0, base_temp + 0.2 * attempt)  # monotonic, never decreases (M3)
682	                params = dict(request.params)
683	                params.update(temperature=temp, system=json_system, _json_mode=True, _seed=attempt)
684	                attempt_req = ReasoningRequest(
685	                    prompt=request.prompt,
686	                    tier=request.tier,
687	                    purpose=request.purpose,
688	                    params=params,
689	                )
690	                # A transient/rate/permanent error is already handled (and bounded by the shared
691	                # deadline) inside _run_with_retries — let it PROPAGATE rather than re-rolling, so
692	                # the budgets do NOT multiply (#102). We re-roll ONLY on a JSON *parse* failure.
693	                text = _run_with_retries(attempt_req, deadline=deadline)
694	                try:
695	                    return extract_json(text)
696	                except ReasoningError as exc:  # parse failure → re-roll with a new seed
697	                    last = exc
698	            raise last if last is not None else ReasoningError("run_json failed")
699	
700	    @staticmethod
701	    def is_available(tier: StakesTier) -> bool:
702	        """Cheap liveness check (spends no LLM turn). Never raises — returns False on error."""
703	        if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
704	            return False
705	        try:
706	            if tier is StakesTier.BULK:
707	                require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
708	                import urllib.request
709	
710	                with urllib.request.urlopen(f"{BULK_BASE_URL}/api/tags", timeout=2) as resp:
711	                    return bool(resp.status == 200)
712	            if tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
713	                import shutil
714	
715	                return shutil.which("claude") is not None and _high_stakes_auth_present()
716	        except Exception:
717	            return False
718	        return False

1	"""Confirmatory-test machinery (METHODOLOGY.md §2, §4).
2	
3	A confirmatory test takes a *locked* hypothesis and returns a :class:`Verdict`. Two
4	mechanisms, selected by hypothesis kind:
5	
6	- unit-level → evaluate on a held-out confirmation partition (Firewall B via
7	  :class:`DiscoverConfirmSplit`);
8	- whole-corpus → compare the observed statistic against a null/permutation distribution and
9	  require stability across resamples.
10	
11	This module defines the *contract and guards* around confirmation. The concrete statistics
12	are supplied by callers via the baseline toolkit; the engine's responsibility here is to
13	refuse to confirm an unlocked hypothesis or on discovery data, validate the supplied
14	statistics, and map an outcome onto one of the three verdicts honestly (including
15	``indeterminate``).
16	
17	Direction handling (audit pass 1, issue #2): a claim predicts the statistic lies in a
18	particular tail of the null. The empirical p-value is computed against *that* tail, so a
19	claim predicting a low value is confirmable, and a genuine contradiction (significant in the
20	opposite tail) is detectable — both were impossible under the previous direction-blind
21	upper-tail-only implementation.
22	"""
23	
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import datetime as _dt
27	import math
28	import warnings
29	from typing import Iterable, Sequence
30	
31	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import DiscoverConfirmSplit, FirewallViolation
32	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Direction, Hypothesis, HypothesisKind
33	from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import (
34	    TimestampAuthority,
35	    require_preregistered,
36	)
37	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict
38	
39	
40	def _gate_preregistration(hypothesis: Hypothesis, authority: TimestampAuthority | None) -> None:
41	    """Pre-registration gate for the confirm primitives.
42	
43	    If ``authority`` is supplied, run the full :func:`require_preregistered` check (content
44	    binding + verifiable timestamp + lock-before-this-moment) — so a study calling a confirm
45	    primitive *directly* (outside a runner) gets the same guarantee the runners enforce. If it
46	    is ``None``, fall back to the cheap presence gate (:func:`require_locked`); the caller has
47	    then opted out of real verification and owns that choice.
48	    """
49	    if authority is not None:
50	        require_preregistered(
51	            hypothesis, authority=authority, not_after=_dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc)
52	        )
53	    else:
54	        # Falling back to the cheap presence gate means a hand-set timestamp_proof string passes
55	        # WITHOUT any cryptographic verification. Warn so a direct caller cannot silently skip real
56	        # pre-registration (#G-006); the runners always supply an authority and never hit this.
57	        warnings.warn(
58	            "confirm primitive called with authority=None: pre-registration is verified by a "
59	            "PRESENCE check only (lock fields populated), NOT cryptographically. Pass a "
60	            "TimestampAuthority to verify the proof binds the hypothesis and precedes confirmation.",
61	            stacklevel=3,
62	        )
63	        require_locked(hypothesis)
64	
65	
66	def require_locked(hypothesis: Hypothesis) -> None:
67	    """Cheap **presence** gate: refuse a hypothesis that carries no lock fields at all.
68	
69	    This is defense-in-depth, not the methodology-grade check. It does not verify the proof
70	    binds the content or that the lock precedes confirmation — that is
71	    :func:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.require_preregistered`, which the
72	    confirmatory *runners* (:mod:`adjudication`, :mod:`discovery`) call before they confirm.
73	    A study driving :func:`confirm_whole_corpus`/:func:`confirm_unit_level` directly (outside a
74	    runner) should call ``require_preregistered`` itself to get the real guarantee.
75	    """
76	    if not hypothesis.locked:
77	        raise FirewallViolation(
78	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is not locked; confirm is forbidden "
79	            "before pre-registration (Firewall B / pre-registration)"
80	        )
81	
82	
83	def _validate_alpha(alpha: float) -> None:
84	    # A significance threshold of 0.5 or more is not a meaningful one-sided level, and
85	    # admitting it lets both tails register "significant" at once (alpha < 0.5 guarantees
86	    # p_support + p_contra >= 1 cannot both fall below alpha) — fix-review nit on issue #2.
87	    if not (0.0 < alpha < 0.5):
88	        raise ValueError(f"alpha must be in (0, 0.5); got {alpha}")
89	
90	
91	def _validate_pvalue(p_value: float) -> None:
92	    if not (0.0 <= p_value <= 1.0):
93	        raise ValueError(f"p_value must be in [0, 1]; got {p_value}")
94	
95	
96	def _validate_finite(name: str, value: float) -> None:
97	    if not math.isfinite(value):
98	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be a finite number; got {value}")
99	
100	
101	def verdict_from_pvalue(
102	    hypothesis_id: str,
103	    *,
104	    statistic: float,
105	    p_value: float,
106	    alpha: float,
107	    powered: bool,
108	    direction_supports_claim: bool,
109	    test_description: str = "confirmatory test",
110	) -> Verdict:
111	    """Map a confirmatory statistic onto the three verdicts.
112	
113	    - not ``powered`` → ``indeterminate`` (the method cannot decide).
114	    - significant and direction agrees with the claim → ``supported``.
115	    - significant and direction disagrees → ``contradicted``.
116	    - not significant → ``indeterminate`` (absence of evidence is not contradiction here).
117	
118	    ``p_value`` and ``direction_supports_claim`` must be derived by the caller from a test
119	    appropriate to the claim's predicted direction. For a unit-level held-out test the engine
120	    is handed only an opaque ``statistic`` + ``p_value`` and cannot itself infer whether the
121	    statistic supports or contradicts the claim — so ``direction_supports_claim`` is a trusted
122	    caller obligation, ``statistic`` is recorded for provenance only (it does NOT gate the
123	    verdict), and the deciding flag is recorded in ``evidence`` so the verdict is re-derivable
124	    (audit pass 2, issue #20). Inputs are validated (issue #4) and the recorded decision rule
125	    reflects the caller's ``test_description``, not a hard-coded sidedness label (issue #2).
126	    """
127	    _validate_pvalue(p_value)
128	    _validate_alpha(alpha)
129	    _validate_finite("statistic", statistic)
130	
131	    rule = f"{test_description} at alpha={alpha}; powered={powered}"
132	    common = dict(
133	        statistic=statistic,
134	        threshold=alpha,
135	        evidence={"p_value": p_value, "direction_supports_claim": direction_supports_claim},
136	    )
137	    if not powered:
138	        return Verdict.indeterminate(hypothesis_id, rule, notes="underpowered", **common)
139	    if p_value <= alpha:
140	        if direction_supports_claim:
141	            return Verdict.supported(hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
142	        return Verdict.contradicted(hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
143	    return Verdict.indeterminate(hypothesis_id, rule, notes="not significant at alpha", **common)
144	
145	
146	def confirm_unit_level(
147	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
148	    *,
149	    split: DiscoverConfirmSplit,
150	    evaluated_ids: Iterable[str],
151	    statistic: float,
152	    p_value: float,
153	    alpha: float,
154	    powered: bool,
155	    direction_supports_claim: bool,
156	    authority: TimestampAuthority | None = None,
157	) -> Verdict:
158	    """Confirm a unit-level hypothesis on the held-out partition only.
159	
160	    Pass ``authority`` to verify pre-registration in full (content binding + timestamp +
161	    lock-before-now); omit it to fall back to the presence gate (see :func:`require_locked`).
162	
163	    Direction (pass 4, #G-017): unlike :func:`confirm_whole_corpus` — where the verdict polarity
164	    is derived from the pre-registered ``predicted_direction`` tail — the unit-level verdict is
165	    governed SOLELY by the caller-supplied ``direction_supports_claim`` flag (the engine sees only
166	    an opaque held-out ``statistic``/``p_value`` and cannot itself infer the tail). The
167	    hypothesis's ``predicted_direction`` is therefore advisory at unit level: it is shape-validated
168	    below for safety but does NOT decide the verdict. A study that wants the locked tail to bind the
169	    unit-level decision must derive ``direction_supports_claim`` from it in its own confirmer.
170	    """
171	    if hypothesis.kind is not HypothesisKind.UNIT_LEVEL:
172	        raise ValueError("confirm_unit_level requires a UNIT_LEVEL hypothesis")
173	    # Defense-in-depth direction validation (pass 3, #F-002): the unit-level path takes the
174	    # deciding direction as a trusted caller flag (``direction_supports_claim``) and so never
175	    # routed through ``_resolve_direction`` — which left a locked-but-invalid
176	    # ``predicted_direction`` (e.g. "UP") entirely unchecked here. ``Hypothesis.__post_init__``
177	    # now rejects such values at construction, but re-validate any pre-registered direction here
178	    # so a hypothesis constructed by an unusual path (or a future field mutation) cannot confirm
179	    # against an unrecognized tail.
180	    if hypothesis.predicted_direction is not None and (
181	        hypothesis.predicted_direction not in _VALID_DIRECTIONS
182	    ):
183	        raise ValueError(
184	            f"predicted_direction must be one of {_VALID_DIRECTIONS}; got "
185	            f"{hypothesis.predicted_direction!r}"
186	        )
187	    _gate_preregistration(hypothesis, authority)
188	    split.assert_confirm_only(evaluated_ids)  # Firewall B (rejects empty / discovery ids)
189	    return verdict_from_pvalue(
190	        hypothesis.hypothesis_id,
191	        statistic=statistic,
192	        p_value=p_value,
193	        alpha=alpha,
194	        powered=powered,
195	        direction_supports_claim=direction_supports_claim,
196	        test_description="held-out unit-level test",
197	    )
198	
199	
200	def _empirical_p(null: Sequence[float], observed: float, tail: Direction) -> float:
201	    """One-sided empirical p-value with +1 smoothing for the given tail."""
202	    n = len(null)
203	    if tail == "greater":
204	        extreme = sum(1 for v in null if v >= observed)
205	    else:
206	        extreme = sum(1 for v in null if v <= observed)
207	    return (extreme + 1) / (n + 1)
208	
209	
210	def _resample_stability(
211	    resamples: Sequence[float], null: Sequence[float], tail: Direction, alpha: float
212	) -> float:
213	    """Fraction of resamples that **reproduce the observed effect**.
214	
215	    Each resample is scored as its own observed statistic against the null and must itself be
216	    significant in the predicted tail (empirical p ≤ ``alpha``). This measures reproduction of
217	    the observed *effect* — not merely landing on the predicted side of the null center, which a
218	    resample distribution clearing the null median by an epsilon would satisfy while reproducing
219	    nothing of the observed magnitude (audit pass 2, #139). The closer to 1.0, the more
220	    consistently the effect's significance reproduces across resamples.
221	
222	    The default path is O(R·N) pure Python (one null pass per resample). When numpy is available
223	    (the optional ``baseline`` extra) the whole R×N comparison runs as a single vectorized op
224	    (pass 3, #F-017). The numpy path computes the IDENTICAL integer extreme-counts and the same
225	    ``(extreme + 1) / (n + 1) <= alpha`` test, so its result is exactly equal to the Python path;
226	    it is purely a speed optimization for large R·N, never a semantic change.
227	    """
228	    n = len(null)
229	    try:
230	        import numpy as np
231	
232	        # Use searchsorted on a sorted null (O((R+N) log N) time, O(R) memory) rather than the
233	        # R×N broadcast boolean matrix (#G-010): the matrix peaks at ~R*N bytes (250MB for
234	        # R=5000,N=50000) and can OOM where the pure-Python loop would not. The integer
235	        # extreme-counts are IDENTICAL to _empirical_p: for 'greater', count(null >= r) = N -
236	        # searchsorted(sorted, r, 'left'); for 'less', count(null <= r) = searchsorted(sorted, r,
237	        # 'right'). Then (extreme+1)/(n+1) <= alpha, same as the pure path.
238	        sorted_null = np.sort(np.asarray(null, dtype=np.float64))
239	        res_arr = np.asarray(resamples, dtype=np.float64)
240	        if tail == "greater":
241	            extreme = n - np.searchsorted(sorted_null, res_arr, side="left")
242	        else:
243	            extreme = np.searchsorted(sorted_null, res_arr, side="right")
244	        p = (extreme + 1) / (n + 1)
245	        return float(int((p <= alpha).sum()) / len(resamples))
246	    except ImportError:
247	        significant = sum(1 for r in resamples if _empirical_p(null, r, tail) <= alpha)
248	        return significant / len(resamples)
249	
250	
251	_VALID_DIRECTIONS = ("greater", "less")
252	
253	
254	def _resolve_direction(hypothesis: Hypothesis, predicted_direction: Direction | None) -> Direction:
255	    """Direction is fixed at lock time on the hypothesis (issue #24); the confirm-time argument is
256	    optional and, if given, must MATCH the pre-registered one — it can cross-check, never supply.
257	
258	    A hypothesis reaching here has already passed the confirm gate, so it is locked and its
259	    ``predicted_direction`` is part of the cryptographic binding. A ``None`` locked direction
260	    therefore means the tail was *not* pre-registered; accepting a confirm-time direction in that
261	    case would let the analyst pick the tail AFTER seeing the data (HARKing), flipping
262	    supported↔contradicted on a hypothesis whose lock attests "no direction" (audit pass 4,
263	    #G-001). The tail must be committed before confirmation, so a directionless locked hypothesis
264	    is refused outright rather than completed with a post-hoc direction.
265	
266	    The resolved direction is validated against ``{"greater", "less"}``; any other value is
267	    rejected (an unrecognized tail would silently fall through to 'less' in :func:`_empirical_p`,
268	    flipping the verdict — audit pass 2, #128).
269	    """
270	    locked_dir = hypothesis.predicted_direction
271	    if locked_dir is None:
272	        raise ValueError(
273	            "predicted_direction was not pre-registered: a whole-corpus confirmatory hypothesis "
274	            "must lock its tail ('greater' or 'less') BEFORE confirmation. Supplying a confirm-time "
275	            "direction for a directionless locked hypothesis selects the tail after seeing the data "
276	            "(HARKing) and is refused (#G-001)."
277	        )
278	    if predicted_direction is not None and predicted_direction != locked_dir:
279	        raise ValueError(
280	            "predicted_direction argument contradicts the hypothesis's pre-registered "
281	            f"direction ({predicted_direction!r} != {locked_dir!r})"
282	        )
283	    resolved = locked_dir
284	    if resolved not in _VALID_DIRECTIONS:
285	        raise ValueError(
286	            f"predicted_direction must be one of {_VALID_DIRECTIONS}; got {resolved!r} — an "
287	            "unrecognized tail would silently flip the supported/contradicted verdict"
288	        )
289	    return resolved
290	
291	
292	def confirm_whole_corpus(
293	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
294	    *,
295	    observed: float,
296	    null_distribution: Sequence[float],
297	    alpha: float,
298	    resample_statistics: Sequence[float] | None = None,
299	    stability_threshold: float = 0.9,
300	    predicted_direction: Direction | None = None,
301	    authority: TimestampAuthority | None = None,
302	) -> Verdict:
303	    """Confirm a whole-corpus hypothesis against a null distribution + measured stability.
304	
305	    Pass ``authority`` to verify pre-registration in full (content binding + timestamp +
306	    lock-before-now); omit it to fall back to the presence gate (see :func:`require_locked`).
307	
308	    The predicted tail is taken from the hypothesis's pre-registered ``predicted_direction``
309	    (issue #24). The pattern must (a) beat the null at ``alpha`` in the predicted tail and
310	    (b) be **stable across resamples** — measured by the engine from ``resample_statistics``,
311	    not asserted by the caller (audit pass 2, issue #21). Outcomes:
312	
313	    - significant in the predicted tail AND stable → ``supported``;
314	    - significant in the *opposite* tail → ``contradicted``;
315	    - unstable, not significant, or stability not assessed → ``indeterminate``.
316	
317	    If ``resample_statistics`` is omitted, stability cannot be measured, so ``supported`` is
318	    unavailable and the verdict is ``indeterminate`` ("stability not assessed").
319	    """
320	    if hypothesis.kind is not HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS:
321	        raise ValueError("confirm_whole_corpus requires a WHOLE_CORPUS hypothesis")
322	    _gate_preregistration(hypothesis, authority)
323	    _validate_alpha(alpha)
324	    _validate_finite("observed", observed)
325	    if not null_distribution:
326	        raise ValueError(
327	            "a null/permutation distribution is required for whole-corpus confirmation"
328	        )
329	    for v in null_distribution:
330	        _validate_finite("null_distribution value", v)
331	    if not (0.0 < stability_threshold <= 1.0):
332	        raise ValueError(f"stability_threshold must be in (0, 1]; got {stability_threshold}")
333	
334	    direction = _resolve_direction(hypothesis, predicted_direction)
335	    opposite: Direction = "less" if direction == "greater" else "greater"
336	    p_support = _empirical_p(null_distribution, observed, direction)
337	    p_contra = _empirical_p(null_distribution, observed, opposite)
338	    n = len(null_distribution)
339	
340	    stability: float | None = None
341	    contra_stability: float | None = None
342	    if resample_statistics is not None:
343	        if not resample_statistics:
344	            raise ValueError("resample_statistics, if given, must be non-empty")
345	        for r in resample_statistics:
346	            _validate_finite("resample statistic", r)
347	        stability = _resample_stability(resample_statistics, null_distribution, direction, alpha)
348	        # Also measure how consistently the resamples reproduce the OPPOSITE-tail significance, so a
349	        # contradiction's robustness is recorded in evidence even though (by design) a contradiction
350	        # is reportable without it (#G-005).
351	        contra_stability = _resample_stability(
352	            resample_statistics, null_distribution, opposite, alpha
353	        )
354	    stable = stability is not None and stability >= stability_threshold
355	
356	    rule = (
357	        f"empirical p from {n}-sample null at alpha={alpha}, predicted tail '{direction}'; "
358	        f"stability across resamples >= {stability_threshold} (engine-measured)"
359	    )
360	    evidence = {
361	        "p_support": p_support,
362	        "p_contradict": p_contra,
363	        "null_n": n,
364	        "predicted_direction": direction,
365	        "stability": stability,
366	        "contradiction_stability": contra_stability,
367	        "stability_threshold": stability_threshold,
368	        "n_resamples": len(resample_statistics) if resample_statistics is not None else 0,
369	    }
370	    common = dict(statistic=observed, threshold=alpha, evidence=evidence)
371	
372	    # A contradiction (significant in the OPPOSITE tail) is reportable without a stability gate, and
373	    # this asymmetry with `supported` is INTENTIONAL (#G-005): support is a positive claim that must
374	    # reproduce to be decisive, whereas a statistic landing significantly in the wrong tail is
375	    # evidence OF the opposite — not absence of evidence — so it stands on the observed effect.
376	    # `contradiction_stability` is recorded in evidence (when resamples were supplied) so an auditor
377	    # can see whether the contradiction also reproduces, even though it is not gated on it.
378	    if p_contra <= alpha:
379	        return Verdict.contradicted(
380	            hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="significant in the opposite tail", **common
381	        )
382	    if stability is None:
383	        return Verdict.indeterminate(
384	            hypothesis.hypothesis_id,
385	            rule,
386	            notes="stability not assessed (no resamples supplied)",
387	            **common,
388	        )
389	    if not stable:
390	        return Verdict.indeterminate(
391	            hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="unstable across resamples", **common
392	        )
393	    if p_support <= alpha:
394	        return Verdict.supported(hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
395	    return Verdict.indeterminate(
396	        hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="does not beat null at alpha", **common
397	    )
398

1	"""Adjudication runner — the engine owns the firewall guarantee, not each study (ADR-0008).
2	
3	METHODOLOGY.md describes the adjudication pipeline: build a baseline *blind* to the external
4	claims (Firewall A), convert each claim into a typed, pre-stated hypothesis, test each against
5	the blind baseline, and *score the external source* against the validated baseline (§5). Until
6	now the engine shipped the parts (a custodial :class:`ClaimBlindGuard`, the confirmatory tests,
7	the verdicts) but no composition — so Firewall A held only if a study wired it correctly by
8	hand. For a blueprint inherited by many studies, that pushes the single most important
9	methodological guarantee onto every cloner, where it is easy to get silently wrong (build the
10	baseline with the claims in scope and nothing stops you).
11	
12	:func:`adjudicate` makes the guarantee structural: it builds the baseline *inside* a sealed
13	claim-guard that holds the claims source, so the baseline builder cannot reach the claims
14	during construction (any attempt raises ``FirewallViolation``); only after the baseline is
15	built does the runner release the claims and adjudicate. It then aggregates the verdicts into a
16	:class:`SourceScorecard` — the §5 "score the source" step, expressed as alignment *frequency*
17	on decisive claims, explicitly NOT a normative judgement of the source's quality (richer
18	dimensions — where it aligns, diverges, leaves gaps — are data-surfaced downstream, per §5).
19	"""
20	
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import datetime as _dt
24	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
25	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Callable, Iterable, Protocol
26	
27	from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ClaimRecord, ExternalClaimsSource
28	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
29	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import ClaimBlindGuard, FirewallViolation
30	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis, HypothesisOrigin
31	from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import (
32	    TimestampAuthority,
33	    require_preregistered,
34	)
35	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict, VerdictLabel
36	
37	if TYPE_CHECKING:
38	    # Annotations only — importing baseline at runtime would invert the layering
39	    # (baseline.toolkit depends on methodology.firewalls) and create a cycle.
40	    from assay_engine.baseline.toolkit import BaselineArtifact, BaselineBuilder
41	
42	
43	class ClaimConfirmer(Protocol):
44	    """Study-supplied: confirm one claim-derived hypothesis against the (blind) baseline.
45	
46	    The study owns this because only it knows the baseline's structure and how a given claim
47	    maps onto a measurement of it; the engine supplies the confirmatory primitives
48	    (``confirm_whole_corpus`` / ``confirm_unit_level``) the study uses inside it.
49	    """
50	
51	    def __call__(
52	        self, hypothesis: Hypothesis, baseline: BaselineArtifact, claim: ClaimRecord
53	    ) -> Verdict: ...
54	
55	
56	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
57	class SourceScorecard:
58	    """How an external source aligns with the independent baseline (METHODOLOGY.md §5).
59	
60	    ``alignment_rate`` is supported / (supported + contradicted) — the fraction of *decisive*
61	    claims where the blind baseline agrees with the source's assertion. ``indeterminate`` is
62	    excluded from the denominator (the method could not decide; counting it would understate
63	    or overstate alignment). This is a frequency, not a verdict on whether the source is
64	    "good": it says how often an independent reading of the data corroborates the source.
65	    """
66	
67	    source: str
68	    n_supported: int
69	    n_contradicted: int
70	    n_indeterminate: int
71	    alignment_rate: float | None
72	    verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...] = field(default_factory=tuple)
73	
74	    @property
75	    def total(self) -> int:
76	        return self.n_supported + self.n_contradicted + self.n_indeterminate
77	
78	    @property
79	    def decisive(self) -> int:
80	        return self.n_supported + self.n_contradicted
81	
82	
83	def _score(source: str, verdicts: list[Verdict]) -> SourceScorecard:
84	    counts = {
85	        VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED: 0,
86	        VerdictLabel.CONTRADICTED: 0,
87	        VerdictLabel.INDETERMINATE: 0,
88	    }
89	    for v in verdicts:
90	        counts[v.label] += 1
91	    decisive = counts[VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED] + counts[VerdictLabel.CONTRADICTED]
92	    rate = counts[VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED] / decisive if decisive else None
93	    return SourceScorecard(
94	        source=source,
95	        n_supported=counts[VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED],
96	        n_contradicted=counts[VerdictLabel.CONTRADICTED],
97	        n_indeterminate=counts[VerdictLabel.INDETERMINATE],
98	        alignment_rate=rate,
99	        verdicts=tuple(verdicts),
100	    )
101	
102	
103	def adjudicate(
104	    corpus: Corpus,
105	    claims_source: ExternalClaimsSource,
106	    *,
107	    baseline_builder: BaselineBuilder,
108	    hypothesis_for: Callable[[ClaimRecord], Hypothesis],
109	    confirm: ClaimConfirmer,
110	    authority: TimestampAuthority,
111	    source_name: str = "external",
112	) -> tuple[BaselineArtifact, SourceScorecard]:
113	    """Run the full adjudication, enforcing Firewall A by construction.
114	
115	    1. The claims source is kept in this function's local scope and is NOT handed to the
116	       builder; the baseline is built inside a sealed :class:`ClaimBlindGuard` that holds
117	       nothing. This is a **signature-level** guarantee: the builder is never *handed* a
118	       claims source by the engine, so it cannot accidentally consult the claims. It is NOT
119	       frame isolation — a builder that deliberately reflects into this runner's call stack
120	       (``sys._getframe``) could reach ``claims_source``; preventing that would require
121	       running the builder in a separate process, which is out of scope. The guarantee is
122	       against *accidental* circularity, not against a builder that willfully defeats the
123	       firewall.
124	    2. After the baseline exists, each claim is mapped (by ``hypothesis_for``) to its typed,
125	       pre-stated ``EXTERNAL_CLAIM`` hypothesis whose ``source_claim_id`` must match the claim
126	       and which must be genuinely **pre-registered** — :func:`~assay_engine.methodology.
127	       preregistration.require_preregistered` checks (via the supplied ``authority``) that the
128	       proof binds this hypothesis's content and that its attested lock time precedes the
129	       **baseline build** (the ordering instant is captured before the build). A claim-derived
130	       hypothesis must therefore be locked *before* ``adjudicate`` is called — ``hypothesis_for``
131	       looks up an already-locked hypothesis; locking on demand inside it (after the baseline
132	       exists) is rejected, so the hypothesis cannot have been tuned to the baseline.
133	    3. The verdict's ``hypothesis_id`` must match the hypothesis it answers (no misattribution),
134	       and the verdicts are aggregated into a :class:`SourceScorecard`.
135	
136	    ``authority`` is the pre-registration timestamp authority (e.g.
137	    :class:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.LocalHmacAuthority`, or an RFC-3161
138	    adapter); the engine ships no silent-accept default, so a study must supply a real one.
139	
140	    Returns ``(baseline, scorecard)``. Raises ``FirewallViolation`` (incl.
141	    :class:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.PreRegistrationError`) on any
142	    claim↔hypothesis↔verdict identity mismatch, a non-``EXTERNAL_CLAIM`` (data-surfaced)
143	    hypothesis, or a hypothesis that is not verifiably pre-registered before confirmation.
144	    """
145	    # The claims source stays in THIS function's local scope and is never given to the builder.
146	    # The builder receives a guard holding nothing (a blind-mode signal only), so there is no
147	    # object reachable from the builder that contains the claims — not even via the guard's
148	    # internals. This is stronger than a custodial guard, whose private `_claims_source` a
149	    # determined builder could read past the sealed `release()` check (adversarial review).
150	    # Captured BEFORE the baseline is built: an EXTERNAL_CLAIM hypothesis derives from the
151	    # external claim, not from the baseline, so it must be pre-registered before the baseline (the
152	    # answer) exists. Using this as `not_after` makes "lock before the result is in hand" real —
153	    # a hypothesis locked on demand inside `hypothesis_for` (i.e. after the build) is rejected,
154	    # forcing studies to pre-lock their claim-derived hypotheses (#83).
155	    not_after = _dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc)
156	
157	    guard: ClaimBlindGuard[ExternalClaimsSource] = ClaimBlindGuard()
158	    with guard.sealed():
159	        baseline = baseline_builder.build(corpus, claim_guard=guard)
160	
161	    # Materialize and guard empty here (pass 3, #F-010): the pipeline runner rejects an empty
162	    # claim set as a misconfiguration, but the standalone entry point silently returned a vacuous
163	    # total=0 scorecard — an inconsistent contract where one caller errors and the other does
164	    # not. Fail loud uniformly: adjudicating zero claims scores nothing and is never intended.
165	    materialized = list(claims_source.claims())
166	    if not materialized:
167	        raise ValueError(
168	            "adjudication requires at least one claim; the claims source yielded none "
169	            "(nothing to adjudicate)"
170	        )
171	
172	    scorecard = adjudicate_with_baseline(
173	        baseline,
174	        materialized,
175	        hypothesis_for=hypothesis_for,
176	        confirm=confirm,
177	        authority=authority,
178	        not_after=not_after,
179	        source_name=source_name,
180	    )
181	    return baseline, scorecard
182	
183	
184	def adjudicate_with_baseline(
185	    baseline: BaselineArtifact,
186	    claims: "Iterable[ClaimRecord]",
187	    *,
188	    hypothesis_for: Callable[[ClaimRecord], Hypothesis],
189	    confirm: ClaimConfirmer,
190	    authority: TimestampAuthority,
191	    not_after: _dt.datetime,
192	    source_name: str = "external",
193	    on_step: "Callable[[Hypothesis, Verdict], None] | None" = None,
194	) -> SourceScorecard:
195	    """Adjudicate a **materialized claim set** against an already-built blind baseline (core).
196	
197	    Factored out of :func:`adjudicate` so a composed pipeline that builds the baseline once
198	    (and shares it with discovery) can reuse the identical, hardened Firewall-A loop instead of
199	    rebuilding the baseline. The caller is responsible for having built ``baseline`` blind and
200	    for passing the ``not_after`` ordering instant captured *before* that build — so the
201	    lock-before-baseline guarantee is preserved across the shared-baseline composition.
202	
203	    ``claims`` is **materialized once** here (not re-pulled from a source): a caller that gated
204	    or recorded a claim snapshot must adjudicate *that* snapshot, not a set a re-iterable source
205	    could change between the gate and the scoring (adversarial review #95). Enforces the same
206	    claim↔hypothesis↔verdict identity + pre-registration checks as :func:`adjudicate`.
207	    """
208	    verdicts: list[Verdict] = []
209	    materialized = list(claims)  # claims used only after the blind baseline is built
210	    # within-run identity uniqueness: a repeated claim_id would be counted once per occurrence in
211	    # _score, inflating the scorecard denominator and biasing alignment_rate (#138).
212	    seen_claim_ids: set[str] = set()
213	    for c in materialized:
214	        if c.claim_id in seen_claim_ids:
215	            raise FirewallViolation(
216	                f"duplicate claim_id {c.claim_id!r} within one adjudication run — claim identity "
217	                "must be unique (a repeat would inflate the scorecard denominator)"
218	            )
219	        seen_claim_ids.add(c.claim_id)
220	    # within-run hypothesis uniqueness (pass 3, #F-019): two *distinct* claims whose
221	    # ``hypothesis_for`` maps to the same hypothesis_id would each contribute a verdict for that
222	    # one hypothesis, double-counting it in the scorecard denominator and biasing alignment_rate.
223	    # The claim_id guard above cannot catch this (the claim_ids differ); guard the hypothesis_id
224	    # separately so one hypothesis decides the source's alignment exactly once.
225	    seen_hypothesis_ids: set[str] = set()
226	    for claim in materialized:
227	        hypothesis = hypothesis_for(claim)
228	        if hypothesis.hypothesis_id in seen_hypothesis_ids:
229	            raise FirewallViolation(
230	                f"duplicate hypothesis_id {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} produced for claim "
231	                f"{claim.claim_id!r} — two claims map to one hypothesis, which would "
232	                "double-count it in the scorecard"
233	            )
234	        seen_hypothesis_ids.add(hypothesis.hypothesis_id)
235	        if hypothesis.origin is not HypothesisOrigin.EXTERNAL_CLAIM:
236	            raise FirewallViolation(
237	                f"claim {claim.claim_id!r} produced a {hypothesis.origin.value!r} hypothesis; "
238	                "adjudicated claims must be EXTERNAL_CLAIM (pre-stated, not data-surfaced)"
239	            )
240	        if hypothesis.source_claim_id != claim.claim_id:
241	            raise FirewallViolation(
242	                f"hypothesis for claim {claim.claim_id!r} carries source_claim_id "
243	                f"{hypothesis.source_claim_id!r} — claim↔hypothesis misattribution"
244	            )
245	        # Pre-registration: the proof must bind THIS hypothesis's content (no post-lock content
246	        # swap) and its attested lock time must precede the baseline build (so the claim-derived
247	        # hypothesis cannot have been tuned to the baseline).
248	        require_preregistered(hypothesis, authority=authority, not_after=not_after)
249	        verdict = confirm(hypothesis, baseline, claim)
250	        # A confirmer that forgets to `return` yields None; accessing verdict.hypothesis_id
251	        # would raise an opaque AttributeError instead of a typed firewall error (pass 3,
252	        # #F-021). Guard the contract explicitly.
253	        if not isinstance(verdict, Verdict):
254	            raise FirewallViolation(
255	                f"confirm returned {type(verdict).__name__} for hypothesis "
256	                f"{hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} — expected a Verdict"
257	            )
258	        if verdict.hypothesis_id != hypothesis.hypothesis_id:
259	            raise FirewallViolation(
260	                f"verdict for hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} reports hypothesis_id "
261	                f"{verdict.hypothesis_id!r} — hypothesis↔verdict misattribution"
262	            )
263	        verdicts.append(verdict)
264	        if on_step is not None:
265	            # per-claim hook (in order) so a caller can record the pre-registered hypothesis AND
266	            # its verdict to provenance as each happens, not in a post-hoc batch (#93).
267	            on_step(hypothesis, verdict)
268	
269	    return _score(source_name, verdicts)
270
